DISCOVERY IS STAYED AS TO DEFENDANT FOR 120 DAYS PENDING RESOLUTION OF THE RELATED CRIMINAL CASE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay This Action Pending Resolution Of Criminal Matter Or, In The Alternative, To Stay Discovery
Off Calendar Sep-15-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. JASON A. POLLACK, ESQ. of Law Offices of Jason Pollack, Tel No. 310-824-8733 appearing telephonically for the Plaintiffs;
Atty. STEVEN L. HAMMOND, ESQ. of Cornerstone Law Group, Tel No. 415-230-8705 appearing for Defendant Ian Duggan.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court modified its tentative ruling, and ordered as follows: DEFENDANT IAN DUGGAN'S Motion To Stay This Action Pending Resolution Of Criminal Matter Or, In The Alternative, To Stay Discovery is granted. Discovery is stayed as to defendant for 120 days pending resolution of the related criminal case. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)
